questions.stem:
—I ________ the Great Wall twice. It's amazing.
—Really? When ________ you ________ there last time?
questions.explanation: 选A。第一空'twice'（两次）是现在完成时的标志词，表示过去动作对现在的影响，用have visited；第二空'last time'（上次）是一般过去时的标志，疑问句需借助助动词did，后面动词用原形go。B项第二空时态错误；C项第二空不应使用现在完成时；D项第一空缺少助动词have。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: This is the scientist ________ discovered the new species in the Amazon forest.
questions.explanation: 选A。定语从句先行词是'the scientist'（科学家），指人，且在从句中作主语，关系代词用who。B项which指物；C项whom指人但只能作宾语；D项whose表示'谁的'，在从句中作定语。口诀：先行词是人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who，作定语用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old library ________ by the local government last year, and it looks brand new now.
questions.explanation: 选B。主语'The old library'与动词'repair'是被动关系，需用被动语态；时间状语'last year'表明是一般过去时，因此用一般过去时的被动语态was repaired。A项是一般现在时被动，时态不符；C、D项不是被动语态。被动语态结构：be动词+过去分词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—______ you ________ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ________ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 选B。第一空'yet'（还，已经）是现在完成时的标志词，用于疑问句，结构为Have+主语+过去分词；第二空'an hour ago'（一小时前）是一般过去时的标志，用finished。A项第一空时态错误；C项第二空时态错误；D项两项时态都错误。解题关键：识别时间标志词yet和ago。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It was ________ (fair) for the teacher to give different scores to the same answer. Everyone should be treated equally.
questions.explanation: 选A。根据句意'老师对相同答案给不同分数是不公平的'，需要形容词作表语，unfair（不公平的）符合。B项fairness是名词'公平'；C项fairly是副词'公平地'；D项unfairness是名词'不公平'。前缀un-表示否定，加在fair形容词前构成unfair。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ________ a better plan to solve the environmental problems in our community.
questions.explanation: 选A。come up with意为'想出，提出（计划、主意等）'，符合句意'想出更好的计划'。B项come up意为'走近，被提出'（主语通常是物）；C项come out意为'出版，出现，结果是'；D项come across意为'偶然遇到'。come up with是固定搭配，宾语是plan/idea/solution时常用。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The instructions on the medicine bottle were so ________ that even a child could understand how to take the pills.
questions.explanation: 选A。根据后半句'即使小孩也能理解如何服药'，说明说明书写得'清楚'（clear）。B项difficult（困难的）与句意相反；C项boring（无聊的）和D项similar（相似的）不符合语境逻辑。通过后半句的结果推断前半句的形容词含义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 选A。根据上下文，项目'旨在'（aims）让学校更环保，aim to do sth是固定搭配，意为'旨在做某事'。B项fails（失败）、C项forgets（忘记）、D项refuses（拒绝）均不符合语境。注意主语The project是第三人称单数，动词用aims。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new "Green Campus" project. As a member of the Environmental Club, I was excited to take part in it. The goal is to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

The project (8)______ to make our school more eco-friendly by changing small daily habits. First, we (9)______ to collect plastic bottles in every classroom. We placed special bins in the hallways and explained the plan during morning assemblies. At first, some students didn't understand why we were doing this. They thought it was (10)______ and unnecessary to separate trash. However, after we explained that these bottles would be recycled into new products like clothing and furniture, they became more interested and started helping.

Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to a recycling center near our school. The workers there always praise us for our hard work and dedication. So far, we (12)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic from going into landfills. We are proud that we can help protect the environment through these small but meaningful actions. Our teachers say we are making a real difference.

questions.explanation: 选A。decide to do sth意为'决定做某事'，符合句意'我们决定在每个教室收集塑料瓶'。B项remembered（记得）、C项continued（继续）、D项regretted（后悔）均不符合语境。这是叙述过去发生的事情，用一般过去时decided。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new "Green Campus" project. As a member of the Environmental Club, I was excited to take part in it. The goal is to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

The project (8)______ to make our school more eco-friendly by changing small daily habits. First, we (9)______ to collect plastic bottles in every classroom. We placed special bins in the hallways and explained the plan during morning assemblies. At first, some students didn't understand why we were doing this. They thought it was (10)______ and unnecessary to separate trash. However, after we explained that these bottles would be recycled into new products like clothing and furniture, they became more interested and started helping.

Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to a recycling center near our school. The workers there always praise us for our hard work and dedication. So far, we (12)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic from going into landfills. We are proud that we can help protect the environment through these small but meaningful actions. Our teachers say we are making a real difference.

questions.explanation: 选A。根据and后的unnecessary（不必要的）可知，空格处应填同类负面形容词。boring（无聊的）符合'他们认为垃圾分类既无聊又不必要'。B项dangerous（危险的）、C项expensive（昂贵的）、D项traditional（传统的）均与'分类垃圾'的语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new "Green Campus" project. As a member of the Environmental Club, I was excited to take part in it. The goal is to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

The project (8)______ to make our school more eco-friendly by changing small daily habits. First, we (9)______ to collect plastic bottles in every classroom. We placed special bins in the hallways and explained the plan during morning assemblies. At first, some students didn't understand why we were doing this. They thought it was (10)______ and unnecessary to separate trash. However, after we explained that these bottles would be recycled into new products like clothing and furniture, they became more interested and started helping.

Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to a recycling center near our school. The workers there always praise us for our hard work and dedication. So far, we (12)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic from going into landfills. We are proud that we can help protect the environment through these small but meaningful actions. Our teachers say we are making a real difference.

questions.explanation: 选A。take...to...意为'把……带到……'，符合句意'我们把瓶子带到回收中心'。B项sell（卖）不符合语境（学生是收集后送去回收，不是售卖）；C项lend（借出）、D项show（展示）搭配不当。take sth to sp是常用搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new "Green Campus" project. As a member of the Environmental Club, I was excited to take part in it. The goal is to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

The project (8)______ to make our school more eco-friendly by changing small daily habits. First, we (9)______ to collect plastic bottles in every classroom. We placed special bins in the hallways and explained the plan during morning assemblies. At first, some students didn't understand why we were doing this. They thought it was (10)______ and unnecessary to separate trash. However, after we explained that these bottles would be recycled into new products like clothing and furniture, they became more interested and started helping.

Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to a recycling center near our school. The workers there always praise us for our hard work and dedication. So far, we (12)______ over 500 kilograms of plastic from going into landfills. We are proud that we can help protect the environment through these small but meaningful actions. Our teachers say we are making a real difference.

questions.explanation: 选B。so far（到目前为止）是现在完成时的标志词，表示从过去持续到现在的动作，用have collected。A项是一般过去时，仅表示过去动作；C项是过去完成时，需有过去时间作为参照；D项是过去进行时，表示过去正在进行的动作。注意主语we是第一人称复数，用have。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new "Green Campus" project. As a member of the Environmental Club, I was excited to take part in it. The goal is to reduce waste and teach students about environmental protection.

The project (8)______ to make our school more eco-friendly by changing small daily habits. First, we (9)______ to collect plastic bottles in every classroom. We placed special bins in the hallways and explained the plan during morning assemblies. At first, some students didn't understand why we were doing this. They thought it was (10)______ and unnecessary to separate trash. However, after we explained that these bottles would be recycled into new products like clothing and furniture, they became more interested and started helping.

Every Friday afternoon, we (11)______ the bottles to a recycling center near our school. The workers there always praise us for our hard work and dedication. Our teachers say we are making a real difference.

questions.stem: When was bioplastic first developed according to the passage?
questions.explanation: 选B。细节理解题。根据文章第二段第一句'Scientists first developed bioplastic in the 1990s'（科学家们在20世纪90年代首次开发出生物塑料），可知答案是20世纪90年代。A、C、D项时间与原文不符。细节题解题技巧：根据题干关键词'first developed'定位到原文找对应信息。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of "bioplastic"? It is a new kind of material that looks like plastic but is made from plants like corn or sugarcane. Unlike traditional plastic, which comes from oil and takes hundreds of years to break down, bioplastic can decompose (分解) in just a few months under the right conditions.

Scientists first developed bioplastic in the 1990s, but it was too expensive for widespread use. Now, with advanced technology, the production cost has dropped significantly. Many food companies are starting to use it for packaging sandwiches, drinks, and snacks. Some supermarkets have even replaced their plastic bags with bioplastic ones.

However, bioplastic is not a perfect solution. It needs specific industrial conditions to decompose properly. If it ends up in the ocean or buried deep in a landfill (垃圾填埋场) without oxygen, it might not break down as quickly as expected. Also, growing crops specifically for bioplastic production requires a lot of water and agricultural land, which could otherwise be used for food production.

Despite these challenges, many environmental experts believe bioplastic is still a better choice than traditional plastic for certain uses. "We need to reduce our overall plastic consumption," says Dr. Green, a leading environmental scientist. "But for products that require packaging, bioplastic represents a positive step toward sustainability."

questions.stem: The underlined word "decompose" in Paragraph 1 probably means ________.
questions.explanation: 选C。词义猜测题。根据第一段对比'traditional plastic...takes hundreds of years to break down'（传统塑料需要数百年才能break down）和'bioplastic can decompose in just a few months'，可知decompose与break down（分解）意思相近，因此选'分解'。A项produce（生产）、B项dissolve（溶解，通常指液体中溶解）、D项recycle（回收）均不符合语境。通过对比结构和同义词替换可推断词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of "bioplastic"? It is a new kind of material that looks like plastic but is made from plants like corn or sugarcane. Unlike traditional plastic, which comes from oil and takes hundreds of years to break down, bioplastic can decompose (分解) in just a few months under the right conditions.

Scientists first developed bioplastic in the 1990s, but it was too expensive for widespread use. Now, with advanced technology, the production cost has dropped significantly. Many food companies are starting to use it for packaging sandwiches, drinks, and snacks. Some supermarkets have even replaced their plastic bags with bioplastic ones.

However, bioplastic is not a perfect solution. It needs specific industrial conditions to decompose properly. If it ends up in the ocean or buried deep in a landfill (垃圾填埋场) without oxygen, it might not break down as quickly as expected. Also, growing crops specifically for bioplastic production requires a lot of water and agricultural land, which could otherwise be used for food production.

Despite these challenges, many environmental experts believe bioplastic is still a better choice than traditional plastic for certain uses. "We need to reduce our overall plastic consumption," says Dr. Green, a leading environmental scientist. "But for products that require packaging, bioplastic represents a positive step toward sustainability."

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Green's words in the last paragraph?
options: A. We should stop using all plastics immediately.
B. Bioplastic is the perfect solution to pollution.
C. Reducing plastic use is important, but bioplastic helps.
D. Traditional plastic is better than bioplastic.
questions.explanation: 选C。推理判断题。根据最后一段Dr. Green的话'We need to reduce our overall plastic consumption'（我们需要减少整体塑料消费）和'bioplastic represents a positive step'（生物塑料代表积极的一步），可推断出'减少塑料使用很重要，但生物塑料有帮助'。A项'立即停止使用所有塑料'过于绝对；B项'完美解决方案'与原文'not a perfect solution'矛盾；D项与原文观点相反。推理题需根据原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of "bioplastic"? It is a new kind of material that looks like plastic but is made from plants like corn or sugarcane. Unlike traditional plastic, which comes from oil and takes hundreds of years to break down, bioplastic can decompose (分解) in just a few months under the right conditions.

Scientists first developed bioplastic in the 1990s, but it was too expensive for widespread use. Now, with advanced technology, the production cost has dropped significantly. Many food companies are starting to use it for packaging sandwiches, drinks, and snacks. Some supermarkets have even replaced their plastic bags with bioplastic ones.

However, bioplastic is not a perfect solution. It needs specific industrial conditions to decompose properly. If it ends up in the ocean or buried deep in a landfill (垃圾填埋场) without oxygen, it might not break down as quickly as expected. Also, growing crops specifically for bioplastic production requires a lot of water and agricultural land, which could otherwise be used for food production.

Despite these challenges, many environmental experts believe bioplastic is still a better choice than traditional plastic for certain uses. "We need to reduce our overall plastic consumption," says Dr. Green, a leading environmental scientist. "But for products that require packaging, bioplastic represents a positive step toward sustainability."

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. A New Material Called Bioplastic
C. How to Save the Ocean
D. The Life of an Environmental Scientist
questions.explanation: 选B。主旨大意题。文章围绕生物塑料（bioplastic）展开，介绍了它的定义、历史、优点和缺点，因此'A New Material Called Bioplastic'最能概括全文。A项'塑料的历史'过于宽泛，文章只涉及生物塑料；C项'如何拯救海洋'只是文章提及的一个方面；D项'环境科学家的生活'偏离主题。标题要准确概括文章核心内容，通常主题词会在文中多次出现。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Have you ever heard of "bioplastic"? It is a new kind of material that looks like plastic but is made from plants like corn or sugarcane. Unlike traditional plastic, which comes from oil and takes hundreds of years to break down, bioplastic can decompose (分解) in just a few months under the right conditions.

Scientists first developed bioplastic in the 1990s, but it was too expensive for widespread use. Now, with advanced technology, the production cost has dropped significantly. Many food companies are starting to use it for packaging sandwiches, drinks, and snacks. Some supermarkets have even replaced their plastic bags with bioplastic ones.

However, bioplastic is not a perfect solution. It needs specific industrial conditions to decompose properly. If it ends up in the ocean or buried deep in a landfill (垃圾填埋场) without oxygen, it might not break down as quickly as expected. Also, growing crops specifically for bioplastic production requires a lot of water and agricultural land, which could otherwise be used for food production.

Despite these challenges, many environmental experts believe bioplastic is still a better choice than traditional plastic for certain uses. "We need to reduce our overall plastic consumption," says Dr. Green, a leading environmental scientist. "But for products that require packaging, bioplastic represents a positive step toward sustainability."

options: A. First, find books you enjoy.
B. Second, make it a daily routine.
C. However, reading too fast is not good.
D. Remove any distractions around you.
E. Finally, share what you've read with others.
questions.explanation: 选A。根据后文'Choose books that match your interests'（选择符合你兴趣的书）可知，本段建议'首先，找到你喜欢的书'。A项'First, find books you enjoy'与之对应。B项是第二步建议；D项是关于环境的建议；C、E项与此处语境不符。选句子填空要注意段落主题一致性。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life and schoolwork. However, many middle school students find it difficult to form a good reading habit in their busy schedules. Here are some practical tips to help you become a better reader.

(17)______ Don't force yourself to read classic novels if you prefer science fiction or comics. Choosing books that match your personal interests is the first step to enjoying reading. When you read something you like, time passes quickly.

(18)______ Set aside at least 20 minutes each day for reading, no matter how busy you are. It could be before breakfast, during lunch break, or before bedtime. Consistency matters more than reading for three hours once a month. Making reading a regular part of your routine helps develop a lasting habit.

Also, find a quiet and comfortable place to read. (19)______ Turn off your phone notifications or put your electronic devices in another room. A peaceful environment allows you to focus deeply on the story or information in the book without interruptions.

(20)______ After finishing an interesting book, try writing a short review or discussing the plot with classmates. Sharing your thoughts not only improves your understanding but also makes reading a more social and enjoyable activity. You might discover new perspectives from others.

options: A. First, find books you enjoy.
B. Second, make it a daily routine.
C. However, reading too fast is not good.
D. Remove any distractions around you.
E. Finally, share what you've read with others.
questions.explanation: 选B。根据后文'Set aside 20 minutes each day'（每天留出20分钟）和'Consistency matters'（坚持很重要）可知，本段建议'第二，养成日常习惯'。B项'Second, make it a daily routine'与之对应。A项已用于第一空；D项是干扰项；C、E项不符合此处逻辑。注意表示顺序的连接词First, Second, Finally等。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life and schoolwork. However, many middle school students find it difficult to form a good reading habit in their busy schedules. Here are some practical tips to help you become a better reader.

(17)______ Don't force yourself to read classic novels if you prefer science fiction or comics. Choosing books that match your personal interests is the first step to enjoying reading. When you read something you like, time passes quickly.

(18)______ Set aside at least 20 minutes each day for reading, no matter how busy you are. It could be before breakfast, during lunch break, or before bedtime. Consistency matters more than reading for three hours once a month. Making reading a regular part of your routine helps develop a lasting habit.

Also, find a quiet and comfortable place to read. (19)______ Turn off your phone notifications or put your electronic devices in another room. A peaceful environment allows you to focus deeply on the story or information in the book without interruptions.

(20)______ After finishing an interesting book, try writing a short review or discussing the plot with classmates. Sharing your thoughts not only improves your understanding but also makes reading a more social and enjoyable activity. You might discover new perspectives from others.

options: A. First, find books you enjoy.
B. Second, make it a daily routine.
C. However, reading too fast is not good.
D. Remove any distractions around you.
E. Finally, share what you've read with others.
questions.explanation: 选D。根据后文'Turn off your phone...put your electronic devices in another room'（关掉手机……把电子设备放在另一个房间）可知，本句建议'移除周围的干扰'。D项'Remove any distractions around you'与之对应。distractions（干扰物）与后文的手机、电子设备对应。A、B项已使用；E项是最后一步；C项与语境无关。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life and schoolwork. However, many middle school students find it difficult to form a good reading habit in their busy schedules. Here are some practical tips to help you become a better reader.

(17)______ Don't force yourself to read classic novels if you prefer science fiction or comics. Choosing books that match your personal interests is the first step to enjoying reading. When you read something you like, time passes quickly.

(18)______ Set aside at least 20 minutes each day for reading, no matter how busy you are. It could be before breakfast, during lunch break, or before bedtime. Consistency matters more than reading for three hours once a month. Making reading a regular part of your routine helps develop a lasting habit.

Also, find a quiet and comfortable place to read. (19)______ Turn off your phone notifications or put your electronic devices in another room. A peaceful environment allows you to focus deeply on the story or information in the book without interruptions.

(20)______ After finishing an interesting book, try writing a short review or discussing the plot with classmates. Sharing your thoughts not only improves your understanding but also makes reading a more social and enjoyable activity. You might discover new perspectives from others.

options: A. First, find books you enjoy.
B. Second, make it a daily routine.
C. However, reading too fast is not good.
D. Remove any distractions around you.
E. Finally, share what you've read with others.
questions.explanation: 选E。根据后文'write down your thoughts or discuss it with friends'（写下想法或与朋友讨论）和'Sharing your thoughts'（分享你的想法）可知，本段建议'最后，与他人分享你读过的内容'。E项'Finally, share what you've read with others'与之对应。注意Finally表示顺序的终结。A、B、D项已使用；C项与分享无关。
